title: Gap #9/M2 increment 3 — the BMF compiler-engine source-rule→runtime crosses four-way; corrects the false 'harness flake' claim
stone: The next M2 floor move: register the two compiler-engine bands that prove the BMF compiler transforms source-rules into the reversible object runtime four-way. These complete the engine floor of M2 beyond the two hard walls (apply-object-rule defunctionalization + symbol-wire, both already merged).
what_proved: bmf-source-rule-to-runtime-band.fk -> 1023 and bmf-component-runtime-exec-band.fk -> 1023, both FOUR-WAY (Go=Rust=TS=fkwu), over engine.fk + compiler.fk + grammar-chars.fk. Manifest rows bmf-source-rule-to-runtime fks 1023, bmf-component-runtime-exec fks 1023.

correction.claim_corrected: The increment-2 evidence (commit_evidence_2026-06-19_bmf_compiler_both_walls.json) and the gap #9 ledger row stated these bands were 'gated on a fourth_prepare_all batch-table-build robustness fix' and that cold parallel builds 'contaminate/empty tables (the BUILD path flakes).' That was WRONG. There is no harness flake.
correction.real_cause: A TEST-INVOCATION artifact. The verification commands used an unquoted shell variable for the prelude list (`./validate.sh $pre form-stdlib/tests/X-band.fk`). The session shell is zsh, which does NOT word-split an unquoted parameter (bash does). So validate.sh's three walkers received the entire prelude string as ONE filename argument and errored ('open form-stdlib/core.fk form-stdlib/grammar-chars.fk ...: no such file or directory'), while the fourth arm (fkwu) reads the band's own `; preludes:` header via fourth_band_srcs and computed the correct verdict. Walkers-errored-vs-fkwu-correct was reported as 'divergent.'
correction.proof_of_correction: Re-running with the preludes as separate literal args — `./validate.sh form-stdlib/core.fk form-stdlib/grammar-chars.fk form-stdlib/engine.fk form-stdlib/compiler.fk form-stdlib/tests/bmf-source-rule-to-runtime-band.fk` — yields '-> 1023, fourth arm: 1 band four-way, 0 divergent.' Same for component-runtime-exec. The harness, the kernels, and the bands were always correct.
correction.implication: The 'resource saturation / batch-build contention' conclusion from the prior session was also this zsh word-splitting bug, not a real harness condition. No fourth_prepare_all fix is needed (the dismissed chip task_46a9362f stands; its stated reason was wrong but the conclusion — no harness fix — holds). Lesson: in zsh, always quote prelude-list variables ("$pre") or pass preludes as separate literal args.

commands: cd form && ./validate.sh form-stdlib/core.fk form-stdlib/grammar-chars.fk form-stdlib/engine.fk form-stdlib/compiler.fk form-stdlib/tests/bmf-source-rule-to-runtime-band.fk
cd form && ./validate.sh form-stdlib/core.fk form-stdlib/grammar-chars.fk form-stdlib/engine.fk form-stdlib/compiler.fk form-stdlib/tests/bmf-component-runtime-exec-band.fk
local_validation.summary: Both -> 1023 FOUR-WAY, 'fourth arm: 1 band four-way (fkwu + pre-flattened tables)', 0 divergent. (Bands authored by a prior thread; this registers them four-way.)

remaining: M2 surface still to register four-way: the ~60 language-grammar bands (python-bmf / typescript-bmf / go-bmf / rust-bmf / media grammars), each verified individually since many carry their own host-io / source-compiler / multi-line standing walls; and generated bp-table parity beyond the literal registry floor. The two hard conceptual walls (apply-object-rule defunctionalization + symbol-wire) and the compiler engine (this increment) are now four-way.
north_star: The whole BMF compiler body crystallizes as a lowered recipe walked four-way. Walls + engine are crossed; the remaining surface is per-band registration, no longer blocked by any phantom harness issue.
